senses.definition: used to say that it is better to live a short but meaningful life, filled with courage and boldness, rather than living a long but unremarkable life that lacks purpose or ambition

word_list.word: cowards die many times before their [death]

senses.definition: used to imply that confronting challenges with bravery is better than letting fear hold us back and suffer the consequences of inaction, as it may cause emotional suffering similar to death
used to emphasize the importance of taking action and being willing to take risks in order to achieve great things in life

word_list.word: a bully is always a coward

senses.definition: used to imply that bullies use intimidation to make up for their own feelings of weakness or vulnerability

senses.definition: used to imply that a courageous person will rely on their inner strength and moral character to face challenges, rather than resorting to violence or intimidation

senses.definition: used to imply the importance of embracing the unknown, taking risks, and pushing beyond the boundaries of what is currently known or accepted in order to achieve great things

examples.x: The startup is developing cutting-edge technology, boldly going where no man has gone before in the quest for innovation.

senses.definition: used to suggest that a person who lacks courage or is too timid will never be able to win the affection of the person they desire, and that boldness and perseverance are necessary traits for success in romantic pursuits

examples.x: Even though he was nervous, John mustered up the courage to ask his crush out on a date, remembering the proverb that faint heart never won fair lady.
